claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 16 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 16 and in the 20-week extension period
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant lives in the UK; Child is 16 and in the 20-week extension period; Child lives with claimant; Child is not in local authority care; Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation; Child is not fostered; Child does not work 24+ hours/week; Child has not started an apprenticeship in England; Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right
circumstances: Child is 16 and in the 20-week extension period
Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. To assess this, I'll need to gather some information about you and your children.

Let me start with the first question:

**Do you have any children under 16 years old, or children aged 16-19 who are in approved education or training?**

Please tell me about the children you're inquiring about, including their names and ages.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I'd like to find out if I'm eligible for Child Benefit. I have one child - Alex, who is 16 years old and lives with me at home.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information! Since Alex is 16 years old, I need to find out more about their current situation to determine your eligibility.

**Is Alex currently in approved education or training?** For example, is Alex in school, college, sixth form, or undertaking an apprenticeship or other approved training?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Alex recently left school and has just signed up with the careers service to figure out what to do next.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for clarifying that. This is helpful information.

**Has Alex registered with the careers service within the last 20 weeks since leaving school, or is this a very recent registration?** In other words, approximately how long ago did Alex leave school?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: It's quite recent, yes - within the last 20 weeks. They left school not that long ago and registered with the careers service to get some help deciding on their next steps.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Excellent, thank you for that information. I have one more question to ensure I have the full picture:

**Do you live in the UK, and is Alex living with you in the UK?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in the UK - we're in Manchester. And yes, Alex lives here at home with me.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Perfect! I now have all the information I need to assess your eligibility for Child Benefit.

Based on the rules for Child Benefit, let me explain your situation:

**Your situation:**
- Alex is 16 years old and living with you in the UK
- Alex recently left school and has registered with the careers service within the last 20 weeks

**Why you're eligible:**

According to the Child Benefit rules, you normally qualify if you're responsible for a child under 16 and live in the UK. For children aged 16 or over, you'll normally still qualify for Child Benefit if your child is under 20 and they stay in approved education or training.

Additionally, there's a special provision: you can apply for Child Benefit to continue for 20 weeks if a 16 or 17 year old leaves education or training and registers with a government-sponsored careers service (such as the one Alex has just registered with).

Since Alex has recently registered with the careers service and this is within the 20-week window from leaving school, you remain eligible to claim Child Benefit for Alex during this period.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Great news!** You **are eligible** for Child Benefit for Alex.

This eligibility will continue for the 20-week period from when Alex left school, as long as Alex remains registered with the careers service. If Alex moves into approved education, training, or employment during this period, your eligibility may change, so it's important to keep HMRC updated on any changes in Alex's circumstances.

Is there anything else you'd like to know about Child Benefit?

reasonings: Alex is 16 years old and lives with the claimant in the UK. Although Alex has left school, the claimant can claim Child Benefit for up to 20 weeks after a 16 or 17 year old leaves education or training if they register with a government-sponsored careers service. Alex has recently registered with the careers service within the 20-week eligibility window, making the claimant eligible for Child Benefit.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant is eligible for Child Benefit for Alex. Although Alex is 16 and no longer in full-time education, the recent registration with the careers service within the 20-week post-education window qualifies them for continued Child Benefit eligibility.">

Alex has recently registered with the careers service within the 20-week eligibility window, making the claimant eligible for Child Benefit.

response.overall_reasoning: The claimant is eligible for Child Benefit for Alex. Although Alex is 16 and no longer in full-time education, the recent registration with the careers service within the 20-week post-education window qualifies them for continued Child Benefit eligibility.">
